name: Ventricles
location: Central fluid-filled cavities within the brain (Lateral, Third, and Fourth Ventricles).
description: A set of communicating cavities filled with Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF), produced by the choroid plexus, providing mechanical cushioning, waste clearance, and chemical homeostasis.
functions: Production and circulation of Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF)
clinicalImportance: Obstruction of CSF flow through the cerebral aqueduct causes rapidly progressive intracranial pressure elevation and hydrocephalus.
researchNotes: The glymphatic system drives CSF influx through periarterial space during deep slow-wave sleep to clear beta-amyloid toxins.
interestingFacts: The brain produces approximately 500 mL of CSF daily, completely recycling its volume 3 to 4 times a day.
